Manufactured Hope

Mo Sayyar | May 2026

Manufactured Hope is an interactive reflection on the fragile theatre of choice: two hands, two options, and the quiet tension of deciding when every outcome may already be shaped by forces outside the frame.

The work borrows from the familiar rhythm of a game, but slows it into a suspended political and emotional state. Hope appears, disappears, and reappears as an interface — something offered, performed, withheld, and manufactured.

Mo Sayyar, May 2026
